entities.name: T.C.B.I.A.

entities.name: General Fund Receipt Account 1719
entities.context: Treasury account for rental of land not otherwise classified, where submarginal land revenues to be deposited

entities.name: Bid Form 5-114
entities.context: Form providing that delinquent operation and maintenance charges must be deducted from land sale proceeds

events.description: Chief of Branch of Realty H. M. Critchfield issued memorandum to Area Directors regarding requirement to cite applicable laws and approval dates for easements and rights of way in patent requests
Billings Area Office issued memorandum to all Superintendents regarding rights of way involved in issuance of patents in fee, enclosing copy of December 13, 1954 office letter
Bureau memorandum issued regarding fee patent policy for issuance of patents in fee to competent applicants
Billings Area Office transmitted fee patent policy statement to Superintendents
Billings Area Office transmitted subsequent policy statement to Superintendents
Inter-office transmittal to Superintendent, North Cheyenne, attaching copy of Office memorandum of 11/22/55 re fee patent policy
Office memorandum issued regarding fee patent policy set forth in memorandum of May 16
Billings Area Office memorandum to Superintendents regarding disposition of revenue from submarginal lands, quoting Central Office memorandum of November 23, 1955
Central Office memorandum regarding disposition of submarginal land revenues and amendment to 25 CFR 71.27
Billings Area Office memorandum transmitting committee reports from Land Operations Conference held in Aberdeen Area at Sioux City, Iowa
Billings Area Office memorandum to all Superintendents regarding issuance of patents and deeds subject to collection of unpaid irrigation assessments
Land Operations Conference held in Aberdeen Area, June 13-15, 1955, with committee appointments and reports on reporting systems, soil and range surveys, conservation operations, and permit relations
Committee appointed at Land Operations Conference with K. S. Engel as Chairman, including members from Turtle Mountain, Pierre, Fort Berthold, Cheyenne River, and Standing Rock agencies
Committee made preliminary report to the Land Operations Conference
Federal Register Vol. 20, No. 155, page 5777 contained amendment to 25 CFR 71.27 deleting reference to deposit of fees from submarginal land grazing privileges
Rex Carey, Agency Soil Scientist at Standing Rock, transferred to Pine Ridge with headquarters at Martin

events.location: Pine Ridge, Rosebud, Crow Creek, Lower Brule Reservations
events.description: Range inventory survey made by Soil Conservation Service personnel under T.C.B.I.A. agreement, now considered obsolete due to radical change in concept of scientific range inventory

financial_transactions.description: Grazing privilege fees collected for submarginal lands held in special deposit pending legislation to transfer lands and fees to respective tribes
Revenue from submarginal lands to be deposited to General Fund Receipt Account 1719, Rental of Land Not Otherwise Classified, per amendment to 25 CFR 71.27
Construction charges required by 25 CFR 151 for non-Indian purchasers of Indian land; delinquent operation and maintenance charges deducted from land sale proceeds per bid form 5-114

financial_transactions.amount: 20% of lease rental or permit grazing fee annually
financial_transactions.description: Recommended annual investment in conservation improvements equivalent to 20% of lease rental or permit grazing fee

First lien against irrigable lands on Indian irrigation projects for unpaid construction, operation and maintenance costs per Act of March 7, 1928 (45 Stat. 210); lien to be recited in any patent or instrument issued for such lands

relationships.subject: Bill Smith
relationships.context: Soil Scientist on educational leave, to return next January

relationships.subject: Bureau of Indian Affairs
relationships.context: BLM called attention to issues with patent requests; both agencies involved in processing patents in fee

relationships.subject: Billings Area Office
relationships.context: Area office issuing directives and memoranda to superintendents

relationships.subject: Commissioner of Indian Affairs
relationships.context: Commissioner issuing policy guidance on fee patents

relationships.subject: Soil Conservation Service
relationships.context: SCS performed 1939 range survey under T.C.B.I.A. agreement; planned formal soil survey work plan with BIA for Shannon County

relationships.subject: Indian women married to white men
relationships.context: Committee recommended declaring Indian women married to white men ineligible for grazing allocations as non-Indian husbands effectively receive the subsidy

relationships.subject: lessees/permittees
relationships.context: Lessees and permittees required to comply with conservation plans and pay grazing fees

relationships.subject: Branch of Land Operations
relationships.context: Responsible for stopping accelerated erosion and healing ravages on reservation lands

correspondence.sender_title: Chief, Branch of Realty, Bureau of Indian Affairs
correspondence.recipient: Area Directors, Portland, Aberdeen, California, Billings, and Minneapolis
correspondence.subject: Request for issuance of patents in fee - requirement to cite applicable laws and approval dates for easements and rights of way
correspondence.action_requested: Specify applicable laws and approval dates for easements and rights of way in all patent requests to allow expeditious handling
correspondence.outcome: Memorandum distributed; Billings Area Office forwarded to Superintendents on January 6, 1955

correspondence.subject: Rights of way involved in issuance of patents in fee
correspondence.action_requested: Realty staff to obtain information on rights of way crossing restricted Indian lands and include in transmittals with patent applications or when Indian lands advertised for public sale
correspondence.outcome: Directive issued; information to be obtained from agency files or from organizations to whom rights of way were granted

correspondence.subject: Issuance of patents in fee to competent applicants - policy clarification following numerous objections and inquiries
correspondence.action_requested: All Bureau employees processing or reviewing fee patent applications to be guided by policy in May 16 memorandum and clarifying statement; exceptions to be returned to Superintendent for further action
correspondence.outcome: Policy statement distributed; temporary manual release to be issued incorporating substance of memorandum and statement

correspondence.recipient: Superintendent and Officers in Charge, Billings Area
correspondence.subject: Disposition of revenue from submarginal lands
correspondence.action_requested: Funds held in special deposits to be deposited to Treasury General Fund Receipt Account 1719 before end of fiscal year; future revenues to be deposited to appropriate general fund revenue receipt account
correspondence.outcome: Directive issued quoting Central Office memorandum of November 23, 1955

correspondence.subject: Committee reports, Land Operations Conference, Aberdeen Area
correspondence.action_requested: Comments requested on conclusions reached by several committees at the Land Operations Conference held at Sioux City, Iowa, June 13-15, 1955
correspondence.outcome: Reports attached and distributed for comment

correspondence.recipient: All Superintendents and Officers in Charge
correspondence.subject: Issuance of patents and deeds subject to collection of unpaid irrigation assessments
correspondence.action_requested: Each Superintendent to establish satisfactory checking procedure to ensure compliance with irrigation assessment requirements before land sale transactions are finally completed
correspondence.outcome: Directive issued following Bureau auditors calling attention to failures to obtain payment for accrued charges

correspondence.sender: Billings Area Office, Branch of Realty
correspondence.subject: Fee patent policy - copy of Office memorandum of 11/22/55
correspondence.action_requested: For information - copy of memorandum regarding fee patent policy set forth in memorandum of May 16 and subsequent policy statements of June 3 and October 4, 1955
correspondence.outcome: Transmittal sent with attached memorandum copy

legislative_actions.outcome: Act of March 7, 1928 (45 Stat. 210) enacted, creating first lien against irrigable lands on Indian irrigation projects for unpaid construction, operation and maintenance costs, directing lien to be recited in any patent or instrument issued for such lands

25 CFR 71.27 amended via Federal Register Vol. 20, No. 155, page 5777, deleting reference to deposit of submarginal land grazing fees in special deposits; funds to be deposited to Treasury miscellaneous receipt account
Pending legislation to transfer submarginal lands and fees to respective tribes; not yet enacted as of November 1955; special deposits held in support of this legislation
